title: Inner Speech
description: A multiclass electroencephalography dataset comprising 10 subjects performing inner speech, pronounced speech, and visualized speech tasks. The dataset includes 5,640 trials recorded with 136-channel EEG systems across four directional commands (up, down, left, right). This resource addresses the scarcity of publicly available EEG datasets for inner speech recognition and supports the development of brain-computer interface technologies and investigation of neural mechanisms underlying inner vocalization.
methods_description: Surface electroencephalography was acquired using a 136-channel EEG system from 10 subjects performing three experimental paradigms: inner speech (imagined vocalization), pronounced speech (overt vocalization), and visualized condition (visual imagery). Subjects executed four directional commands (up, down, left, right) across 5,640 total trials.
